# The Problem

## Technical debt

The Bioconductor build system reports 1 FAIL and 7 NOTEs. Nine issues are open, including a data-loss bug in `loadBedGraphTrackFromURL` (#35) where configuration variables — including the URL itself — get overwritten when extra parameters are passed, and five missing igv.js features (CRAM loading, `sortByTag`, sashimi plots, GWAS improvements) explicitly requested by users. I know exactly what needs fixing — I just haven't had the focused time to do it properly.

## Accessibility gap

igvShiny's API is entirely programmatic — to navigate or load a track, you write R code. This is fine for bioinformaticians, but I've seen igvShiny deployed in clinical settings where the developers build the app, but the biologists driving the analysis can't interact with the browser directly. They're passive consumers of whatever the developer hardcoded. No Bioconductor package today offers a natural language interface to a genome browser. I've been wanting to build this for a while — `elmer` finally makes it practical without reinventing the wheel, and the refactoring in M1–M4 gives it a clean API to sit on top of.
